prompt: Japanese ukiyo-e woodblock print, Edo period, in the manner of Hokusai's Thirty-six Views series. A long chain of mountain peaks receding into the distance. The nearest peak is rendered in full dark indigo ink with bold even-weight black outlines. Each successive peak behind it is progressively lighter — less ink, thinner outlines, softer edges — until the farthest peaks are barely whispers of pale grey against a cream sky, almost indistinguishable from the mist. Flat unmodulated color fields with no photographic shading. Diagonal composition from lower-left to upper-right. Visible woodblock texture in the sky. Muted indigo, ochre, pale grey, and cream palette only. No text, no labels, no diagrams, no Western perspective shading, no gradients, no photographic depth, no modern elements. Everything must follow Edo-period woodblock print conventions.
tradition_approach: Hokusai's diagonal landscape composition; nearest peak in full dark indigo with bold even-weight outlines, each successive peak progressively lighter until farthest peaks dissolve into cream mist; flat color fields, indigo-ochre-grey-cream palette.
